tools.name: AlphaFold 1
tools.usage: First-generation neural network predicting inter-residue distances and orientations for ab initio folding.
tools.strengths: Won CASP13; proved deep learning is highly viable for structural biology prediction.
tools.weaknesses: Did not predict atomic coordinates directly; required physical gradient descent to build 3D models.
tools.github_description: AlphaFold 1: original neural network for distance prediction.

tools.name: AlphaFold 2
tools.usage: Atomic-level structure prediction of proteins from sequence and MSAs.
tools.strengths: Outstanding accuracy, massive public database (AFDB), industry gold standard.
tools.weaknesses: High computational requirements, slow MSA search, struggles with non-protein ligands and disordered structures.
tools.github_description: Open source code for AlphaFold 2.

tools.weaknesses: Higher stereochemical error rate on small molecule docking compared to specialized physical solvers.

tools.name: RoseTTAFold2NA
tools.usage: Joint structure prediction of protein-nucleic acid (DNA/RNA) complexes.
tools.strengths: Extends RoseTTAFold's multi-track network to nucleic acids, open weights.
tools.weaknesses: Higher stereochemical error rates for DNA/RNA bonds than specialized molecular dynamics packages.
tools.github_description: RoseTTAFold2NA: open-source protein-nucleic acid complexes predictor.

tools.name: OpenFold
tools.usage: Permissively licensed PyTorch reproduction of AlphaFold2 with full training pipelines.
tools.strengths: Highly trainable from scratch, memory-efficient optimization (up to 4000+ residues on single GPU).
tools.weaknesses: No direct performance speedups in inference, training requires millions of GPU-hours.
tools.github_description: Trainable, memory-efficient, and GPU-friendly PyTorch reproduction of AlphaFold 2

tools.name: Uni-Fold
tools.usage: PyTorch platform for end-to-end training and inference of monomer and multimer folding models.
tools.strengths: First fully open-source multimer code training suite; high performance compatibility with AF2 weights.
tools.weaknesses: Developers-first interface; high computation and storage footprints for cluster execution.
tools.github_description: PyTorch-based platform for end-to-end training and inference of monomer and multimer folding models.

tools.usage: End-to-end open training multimer predictions.
tools.strengths: Optimized multi-chain modeling and sequence alignment processing.
tools.weaknesses: Requires specialized hardware profiles for massive multimeric assemblies.
tools.github_description: Uni-Fold Multimer models.

tools.name: HelixFold
tools.usage: Baidu's PaddlePaddle-based implementation of AlphaFold2 for high-throughput training and inference.
tools.strengths: Drastically reduces training time and cost on specialized NPUs and hardware configurations.
tools.weaknesses: Builds on PaddlePaddle framework, making it less integrated with the standard PyTorch scientific community.
tools.github_description: Baidu's PaddlePaddle-based implementation of AlphaFold2 for high-throughput training and inference.

tools.name: Boltz-1
tools.usage: MIT-licensed, commercial-friendly biomolecular structure prediction (alternative to AF3).
tools.strengths: Permissive MIT license, predicts proteins, RNA, DNA, and ligands, matches AF3 performance on major benchmarks.
tools.weaknesses: Newly released (late 2024), smaller ecosystem, requires post-processing for bond lengths in complexes.
tools.github_description: Official repository for the Boltz biomolecular interaction models

tools.name: FastFold
tools.usage: Inference and training optimizer for AlphaFold/OpenFold models using kernel optimizations and distributed execution.
tools.strengths: Reduces GPU memory footprints, accelerates training throughput up to 3x, scales multimeric inference.
tools.weaknesses: Requires complex environment setup and specialized hardware configurations.
tools.github_description: FastFold: Optimizing training and inference of AlphaFold.

tools.name: LightFold
tools.usage: GPU inference accelerator for OpenFold models using customized attention kernels and model distillation.
tools.strengths: Up to 5x faster structure prediction on standard GPUs for short chains.
tools.weaknesses: Restricted support for massive multimeric complexes, model weights are locked to distilled layouts.
tools.github_description: LightFold inference accelerator.

tools.name: DeepMSA
tools.usage: Automated pipeline for generating multi-source MSAs to feed protein folding neural networks.
tools.strengths: Combines multiple genome databases to find distant sequence homologs, enhancing prediction.
tools.weaknesses: Slower than MMseqs2/ColabFold database pipelines.
tools.github_description: DeepMSA pipeline for generating MSAs.

tools.name: DeepMSA2
tools.usage: Upgraded MSA generator optimizing database searching strategies for AlphaFold2 structure modeling.
tools.strengths: Significantly richer alignments for multi-domain assemblies, higher prediction accuracy on orphan sequences.
tools.weaknesses: High local disk storage footprint for sequence databases.
tools.github_description: DeepMSA2 alignment framework.

tools.name: ColabFold-Local
tools.usage: Local installer script to configure ColabFold pipelines on standalone clusters without Google Colab dependencies.
tools.strengths: Highly robust automated installation, allows localized batch processing on GPU farms.
tools.weaknesses: Requires manual maintenance and updating of databases and conda profiles.
tools.github_description: Local installation configuration for ColabFold.

tools.name: SPEACH_AF
tools.usage: Sampling alternative conformations by introducing synthetic mutations or sequence perturbations into MSAs.
tools.strengths: Forces the network to model minor states and alternative folds, exposing protein dynamics.
tools.weaknesses: Predictions must be verified manually; some configurations yield unphysical intermediates.
tools.github_description: SPEACH_AF conformational sampling package.

tools.name: AF2-Cluster
tools.usage: Sampling alternative states of protein complexes by clustering MSAs before network input.
tools.strengths: Decouples active and inactive conformations of GPCRs, kinases, and transporters.
tools.weaknesses: Heavy computation requirements for multiple parallel clustering inference cycles.
tools.github_description: AF2-Cluster conformational landscape mapping.

tools.name: RFdiffusion
tools.usage: De novo protein backbone generation using structural denoising diffusion models.
tools.strengths: Highly successful de novo binder design, motif scaffolding, symmetric assembly creation.
tools.weaknesses: Only designs backbones; needs external sequence generator (ProteinMPNN) and verification.
tools.github_description: Code for running RFdiffusion

tools.strengths: Extremely fast, robust sequence generation, drastically improves design success rate over physical scoring.
tools.weaknesses: Strictly limited to static input backbones; cannot design backbone topology from scratch.
tools.github_description: Code for the ProteinMPNN paper

tools.strengths: Direct upgrade to ProteinMPNN, allowing interface design for complex non-protein interactions.
tools.weaknesses: Limited to design around pre-positioned static molecules; cannot generate ligand conformations natively.

tools.strengths: End-to-end (backbone + sequence) generation, supports advanced geometric constraints.
tools.weaknesses: Model weights require a commercial API key from Generate:Biomedicines.

tools.name: ColabDesign
tools.usage: Interactive framework for protein backbone/sequence design via Google Colab powered by AF2 and ProteinMPNN.
tools.strengths: Accessible design protocols (binder design, hallucination, fixedbb) with rich visual notebooks.
tools.weaknesses: Primarily notebook-oriented; requires adaptation for non-interactive high-throughput command-line execution.
tools.github_description: Interactive framework for protein backbone/sequence design via Google Colab powered by AF2 and ProteinMPNN.

tools.name: BindCraft
tools.usage: Automated de novo miniprotein binder design combining AlphaFold2, ColabDesign, ProteinMPNN, and PyRosetta/OpenMM.
tools.strengths: High experimental success rate, designs binders for challenging targets in-silico, open-source.
tools.weaknesses: Requires high GPU resources for hallucination/backpropagation, PyRosetta requires commercial license.
tools.github_description: BindCraft: One-shot design of functional protein binders using AlphaFold2, ColabDesign, ProteinMPNN and PyRosetta.

citing_papers.authors: Luis Santiago Mille, Claudia L. Driscoll, John N. Wang et al.

tools.name: Ovo
tools.usage: Modular Nextflow orchestrator and data manager for de novo protein design pipelines (RFdiffusion, ProteinMPNN, AlphaFold validation).
tools.strengths: Infrastructure-agnostic Nextflow scaling, user-friendly CLI and GUI, ProteinQC module for quality control.
tools.weaknesses: Orchestration-only framework; dependent on accuracy and installation of underlying design models.
tools.github_description: Ovo, an Open-Source Ecosystem for De Novo Protein Design.

citing_papers.title: Ovo, an Open-Source Ecosystem for De Novo Protein Design

tools.name: PiFold
tools.usage: Rapid structure-conditioned sequence generation model using 3D node and edge feature projections.
tools.strengths: Faster than ProteinMPNN on single chain structures, excellent recovery of natural sequence motifs.
tools.weaknesses: Limited support for multi-chain assemblies containing non-protein cofactors.
tools.github_description: PiFold: Physics-informed inverse folding.

tools.name: ESM-IF1
tools.usage: Inverse folding model using GNNs to predict sequences matching custom target backbones.
tools.strengths: Pre-trained on millions of structures, integrates sequence likelihood scores to screen stable designs.
tools.weaknesses: Slightly less flexible for loop optimization than ProteinMPNN.
tools.github_description: ESM Inverse Folding model.

tools.name: ProtGPT2
tools.usage: Generative transformer model to output novel de novo sequence profiles based on natural protein space.
tools.strengths: Requires zero input structures; designs highly soluble and folded chains directly from raw generation.
tools.weaknesses: Generates sequences without spatial constraint capabilities.
tools.github_description: ProtGPT2: Deep generative sequence model.

tools.name: ProGen
tools.usage: AI sequence designer generating functional proteins across families controlled by natural specification tags.
tools.strengths: Demonstrated successful wet-lab synthesis of functional lysozymes designed from scratch.
tools.weaknesses: Requires tags for conditioning; does not support 3D geometric constraints natively.
tools.github_description: ProGen sequence design model.

tools.name: ProGen 2
tools.usage: Expanded protein language model suite optimized for de novo sequence creation and fitness landscape prediction.
tools.strengths: Trained on massive multi-genome databases; high sequence viability prediction.
tools.weaknesses: Lacks 3D structural backbone coordination capabilities.
tools.github_description: ProGen 2 sequence generation.

tools.name: EvoDiff
tools.usage: Sequence-based diffusion model generating functional proteins directly without structural inputs.
tools.strengths: Captures structural constraints implicitly; highly viable sequences for disordered structures.
tools.weaknesses: Inference is slower than direct language model autoregressive sampling.
tools.github_description: EvoDiff sequence diffusion.

tools.name: RFdesign
tools.usage: Inpainting and hallucination tool based on RoseTTAFold for building custom binding interfaces.
tools.strengths: Excellent motif scaffolding capabilities.
tools.weaknesses: Preceded by RFdiffusion; slower and less robust poses than current diffusion suites.
tools.github_description: RFdesign: RoseTTAFold-based protein design.

tools.name: ProteinGenerator
tools.usage: End-to-end design generator optimizing backbones and sequences simultaneously via RoseTTAFold checkpoints.
tools.strengths: Direct linkage of sequence and structure during optimization, avoids mismatched folds.
tools.weaknesses: High GPU compute required; optimization can stall in local minima.
tools.github_description: ProteinGenerator simultaneous design model.

tools.name: EvoProtGrad
tools.usage: Directed evolution simulator using gradient-based guidance on protein language models and inverse folding outputs.
tools.strengths: Drastically search-efficient; outputs mutated variants with increased expression or affinity.
tools.weaknesses: Heavy dependence on quality of grading score models (e.g. ESM-1v).
tools.github_description: EvoProtGrad directed design solver.

tools.name: AlphaMissense
tools.usage: Pathogenicity classification of human missense single-nucleotide variants.
tools.strengths: Classified 89% of all human missense variants with 90% confidence, outperforming standard conservation methods.
tools.weaknesses: Outputs database tables; code is reference-only and not structured for new protein predictions.
tools.github_description: Pathogenicity classification of human missense single-nucleotide variants.

github_top_forks.name: zbalsara21/alphamissense
github_top_forks.description: My version of alpha missense

citing_papers.authors: Garyk Brixi, Matthew G. Durrant, Ja‐Lok Ku et al.

tools.name: ESM-1v
tools.usage: Zero-shot prediction of variant effects and mutational fitness landscapes using language model masks.
tools.strengths: Excellent benchmark accuracy for variant engineering, fast scoring.
tools.weaknesses: Does not predict structural consequences directly; sequence-only model.
tools.github_description: ESM-1v variant prediction checkpoints.

tools.name: ESM-2
tools.usage: State-of-the-art transformer representation of evolutionary sequence records.
tools.strengths: Powering ESMFold, outstanding downstream transfer learning features.
tools.weaknesses: Requires massive training datasets, highly parameter-dense.
tools.github_description: ESM-2 evolutionary representation model.

tools.name: VIPER
tools.usage: Visual inspector tool identifying variant clusters and mapping fitness predictions onto 3D structures.
tools.strengths: Highlights functional hotspots in protein assemblies dynamically.
tools.weaknesses: Lacks direct prediction modules; relies entirely on ESM/AlphaFold databases.
tools.github_description: VIPER mutational visualization.

tools.name: SoluProt
tools.usage: Machine learning prediction of protein solubility in E. coli expression systems.
tools.strengths: Highly reliable classification for screening synthetic design candidates.
tools.weaknesses: Lacks structure-level interaction physics.
tools.github_description: SoluProt solubility predictor.

tools.name: MutCompute
tools.usage: CNN classifier trained on protein microenvironments to engineer stabilizing mutations.
tools.strengths: Successfully engineered highly stable PET-depolymerizing enzymes (FAST-PETase).
tools.weaknesses: Limited resolution on highly flexible loop structures.
tools.github_description: MutCompute protein engineering CNN.

tools.name: ESM-Finetune
tools.usage: Finetuning pipelines for adapting ESM-2 checkpoints to specific enzymatic activity data.
tools.strengths: Allows highly customized fitness predictions with very small datasets.
tools.weaknesses: Prone to overfitting if sequence variation is too narrow.
tools.github_description: ESM-Finetune pipeline.

tools.name: DeepSequence
tools.usage: Deep generative latent models (VAEs) predicting mutation effects based on alignment logs.
tools.strengths: Highly accurate zero-shot fitness modeling across diverse protein families.
tools.weaknesses: Requires deep MSAs; inference is relatively slow compared to language models.
tools.github_description: DeepSequence VAE model.

tools.name: Foldseek
tools.usage: Fast, sensitive structural search and alignment against millions of predicted structures in AFDB.
tools.strengths: 10,000x faster than Dali/TM-align, integrates structural alphabet (3D-states) into sequence-based search.
tools.weaknesses: May miss fine-grained structural alignments compared to full rigid coordinate superposition.
tools.github_description: Foldseek enables fast and sensitive comparisons of large structure sets.

github_top_forks.name: gamcil/foldseek
github_top_forks.description: Foldseek enables fast and sensitive comparisons of large structure sets. It reaches sensitivities similar to state-of-the-art structural aligners while being at least 20,000 times faster.

github_top_forks.description: Foldseek enables fast and sensitive comparisons of large structure sets.

github_top_forks.description: Foldseek enables fast and sensitive comparisons of large structure sets.

tools.name: Foldseek Multimer
tools.usage: Searching structural databases using multi-chain assembly configurations.
tools.strengths: Identifies matching quaternary interfaces in milliseconds.
tools.weaknesses: Relies heavily on model coordinate integrity of multimeric inputs.
tools.github_description: Foldseek Multimer alignments.

tools.name: AlphaFind
tools.usage: Fast shape-based structural comparison and search against predictions in the AlphaFold Database.
tools.strengths: Enables discovery of remote structural homologs, intuitive web interface and programmatic API.
tools.weaknesses: Searches only within the pre-computed AlphaFold Database; cannot align arbitrary user-provided structures.
tools.github_description: AlphaFind: shape-based search in structural databases

citing_papers.title: AlphaFind: a web server for structural similarity search of the AlphaFold database

tools.name: AlphaFind v2
tools.usage: Next-generation structural search optimized with advanced embedding models for extreme-speed shape searches.
tools.strengths: Even faster indexing, supports multimeric searches, integrates structural alignments with sequence conservation.
tools.weaknesses: Very newly released preprint status, still in active deployment phase.
tools.github_description: AlphaFind v2: fast shape-based search in structural databases

citing_papers.title: AlphaFind v2: fast shape-based search in structural databases

tools.name: Foldcomp
tools.usage: Highly optimized compression framework for storing and streaming millions of PDB/AlphaFold coordinate files.
tools.strengths: Up to 10x smaller file sizes, allows instantaneous loading of complete proteomes.
tools.weaknesses: Lossy compression (retains backbone and C-beta coordinates only).
tools.github_description: Foldcomp structure compression.

tools.name: USalign
tools.usage: Universal structural alignment program aligning proteins, DNA, RNA, or mixed molecular complexes.
tools.strengths: Outstanding accuracy, handles completely mismatched sequence profiles using TM-score normalization.
tools.weaknesses: Slow on massive databases containing millions of structures compared to Foldseek.
tools.github_description: USalign structural alignment solver.

tools.name: TM-align
tools.usage: Classical structural alignment program computing TM-scores between target chains.
tools.strengths: De facto standard metric for evaluating predicted structural quality.
tools.weaknesses: CPU-bound, not designed for web-scale database searches.
tools.github_description: TM-align structure comparison.

tools.name: Dali
tools.usage: Distance alignment matrix solver mapping structural contacts in 3D space.
tools.strengths: Identifies highly remote structural homology missed by coordinate superposition.
tools.weaknesses: Highly resource-intensive quadratic alignment search.
tools.github_description: Dali structural similarity engine.

tools.name: DiffDock
tools.usage: Diffusion-based blind flexible molecular docking of small molecules to protein target structures.
tools.strengths: High pose prediction accuracy, avoids local minima in binding pockets, handles novel target structures.
tools.weaknesses: Assumes rigid protein structure, can output steric clashes requiring force field minimization.
tools.github_description: Implementation of DiffDock: Diffusion Steps, Twists, and Turns for Molecular Docking

github_top_forks.name: Jnelen/DiffDockHPC
github_top_forks.description: DiffDock implementation that adds support for HPC execution using Slurm and Singularity

github_top_forks.name: labdao/diffdock
github_top_forks.description: Implementation of DiffDock: Diffusion Steps, Twists, and Turns for Molecular Docking

github_top_forks.name: electrojustin/DiffDock-Fine-Tune
github_top_forks.description: Fork of DiffDock designed for fine tuning.

github_top_forks.name: alphacody/DiffDock
github_top_forks.description: Implementation of DiffDock: Diffusion Steps, Twists, and Turns for Molecular Docking

github_top_forks.name: SuperXiang/DiffDock
github_top_forks.description: Implementation of DiffDock: Diffusion Steps, Twists, and Turns for Molecular Docking

tools.name: DiffDock-PP
tools.usage: Rigid protein-protein docking using deep translation/rotation diffusion models.
tools.strengths: Handles huge assemblies quickly, avoids grid search calculations.
tools.weaknesses: Does not model interface side-chain conformational adjustments.
tools.github_description: DiffDock-PP protein docking.

tools.name: DiffDock-L
tools.usage: Docking optimized for peptide and macrocyclic complex binding poses.
tools.strengths: Excellent flexibility handling for large rings.
tools.weaknesses: Requires longer diffusion steps for multi-torsion interfaces.
tools.github_description: DiffDock-L macrocycle poses.

tools.name: FlowDock
tools.usage: Flow matching protein-ligand docking model predicting co-complex layouts directly.
tools.strengths: Outperforms diffusion speeds, natively includes backbone adaptability.
tools.weaknesses: Demands high GPU memory profiles during multi-chain refinement.
tools.github_description: FlowDock flow matching poses.

tools.name: DynamicBind
tools.usage: Flexible deep learning-based docking solver modeling induced-fit pocket changes.
tools.strengths: Handles side-chain coordinate shift predictions during ligand alignment.
tools.weaknesses: Can yield unrealistic conformational states on small pockets.
tools.github_description: DynamicBind flexible pocket solver.

tools.name: EquiBind
tools.usage: Equivariant geometric neural network predicting ligand coordinates in one shot.
tools.strengths: Thousands of times faster than physics solvers, predicts pocket locations implicitly.
tools.weaknesses: Lacks refinement, prone to overlapping atoms without force field post-processing.
tools.github_description: EquiBind one-shot docking.

tools.name: TankBind
tools.usage: Trigonometry-aware neural network prediction for binding poses and affinity grids.
tools.strengths: Accurate coordination of target pockets, stable scoring metrics.
tools.weaknesses: Restricted performance when encountering novel metal cluster interfaces.
tools.github_description: TankBind structure-pose model.

tools.name: FABind
tools.usage: Fast, physics-aware ligand alignment architecture optimizing coordinate loss directly.
tools.strengths: Fast pose identification, robust on large multimeric cavities.
tools.weaknesses: Prone to errors on highly flexible open loops.
tools.github_description: FABind pocket alignment.

tools.name: Uni-Dock
tools.usage: GPU-accelerated molecular docking engine based on AutoDock Vina protocols.
tools.strengths: Massive throughput acceleration (10-50x), scales virtual screening to millions of compounds.
tools.weaknesses: Uses traditional scoring functions rather than deep learning representations.
tools.github_description: Uni-Dock GPU Vina model.

tools.name: AF2Complex
tools.usage: Predicting multi-chain assemblies and screening protein-protein interaction networks using modified AF2 weights.
tools.strengths: Calculates interface metrics (pDockQ) to identify true interaction partners.
tools.weaknesses: Demands huge GPU clusters for comprehensive screening arrays.
tools.github_description: AF2Complex multimer predictor.

tools.name: FoldDock
tools.usage: Docking of protein dimers using custom-built sequence alignment bridges directly inside AF2.
tools.strengths: Simple and fast multimer modeling before AF2-Multimer release.
tools.weaknesses: Struggles with large multimer assemblies (higher than trimers).
tools.github_description: FoldDock dimer docking.

tools.name: AlphaPulldown
tools.usage: Automated pipeline for high-throughput screening of protein-protein interactions (all-against-all or one-against-all).
tools.strengths: Automates MSAs, calculates interface scores, exports results in formats ready for Cytoscape.
tools.weaknesses: Inference computation cost is high for large screening grids.
tools.github_description: AlphaPulldown interaction screening.

tools.name: IgFold
tools.usage: Antibody structure prediction using pre-trained language models and specialized structural targets.
tools.strengths: Outstanding accuracy on flexible antibody loops (H3 loops), predicts structures in seconds.
tools.weaknesses: Restricted to immunoglobulin variable domains; cannot fold general proteins.
tools.github_description: IgFold antibody predictor.

tools.name: LYRA
tools.usage: Antibody and T-cell receptor (TCR) structural modeling tool incorporating specialized templates.
tools.strengths: Fast modeling, incorporates canonical loop conformations.
tools.weaknesses: Lacks direct end-to-end deep learning backpropagation.
tools.github_description: LYRA TCR/Antibody modeler.

tools.name: AbFold
tools.usage: Specialized antibody folding model utilizing template-matching constraints in conjunction with ESM weights.
tools.strengths: Highly accurate CDR loop structures, handles nanobodies.
tools.weaknesses: Slower than IgFold due to physics-based template checks.
tools.github_description: AbFold antibody folding system.

tools.name: NanobodyFold
tools.usage: Refining and folding nanobody architectures with specific variable region parameters.
tools.strengths: Optimized for single-chain VHH designs.
tools.weaknesses: Limited capabilities when modeling complex multimeric antibody formats.
tools.github_description: NanobodyFold VHH modeler.

tools.name: TCR-Fold
tools.usage: Modeling T-cell receptor (TCR) binding loops and TCR-peptide assemblies.
tools.strengths: Provides critical annotations for immunotherapeutic development.
tools.weaknesses: Lower modeling accuracy on highly dynamic TCR-MHC interfaces.
tools.github_description: TCR-Fold receptor modeler.

tools.name: AlphaTCR
tools.usage: Predicting TCR-peptide-MHC interactions using structural embedding metrics.
tools.strengths: Integrates structural parameters directly with immunological screen logs.
tools.weaknesses: High rate of false positives in cross-reactive binding assays.
tools.github_description: AlphaTCR screening framework.

tools.name: RFantibody
tools.usage: Antibody design using adapted RoseTTAFold weights to design variable loop scaffolds.
tools.strengths: Integrates with RFdiffusion for de novo antibody binder generation.
tools.weaknesses: CDR loop design success rates are lower than standard RFdiffusion binder pipelines.
tools.github_description: RFantibody designer.

tools.name: DiffAb
tools.usage: Generative model optimizing antibody sequence and structure jointly using 3D diffusion coordinates.
tools.strengths: Excellent pose viability on target CDR loops.
tools.weaknesses: Requires force-field post-processing to correct stereochemical bond angles.
tools.github_description: DiffAb antibody diffusion solver.

tools.name: ROCKET
tools.usage: Inference-time refinement of OpenFold predicted structures against Cryo-EM maps or X-ray crystallography data.
tools.strengths: Optimizes network latent space using differentiable likelihood targets, captures dynamic domain shifts.
tools.weaknesses: Tethered to OpenFold weights, requires raw density map/diffraction data and high GPU computational power.
tools.github_description: ROCKET: Refining OpenFold with Crystallographic/Cryo-EM liKElihood Targets.

tools.name: Phenix-AlphaFold
tools.usage: Integrating AlphaFold models into crystallographic molecular replacement and refinement pipelines inside Phenix.
tools.strengths: Automates template trimming, solves challenging molecular replacement cases where traditional structures fail.
tools.weaknesses: Proprietary license components inside Phenix; requires local installations.
tools.github_description: Phenix structural biology integration.

tools.name: CryoREAD
tools.usage: Automated de novo building of proteins and nucleic acids directly from Cryo-EM density maps.
tools.strengths: Extremely fast structure building, performs well at medium to low resolutions.
tools.weaknesses: Relies on density map quality; may misassign sequence identities in local regions.
tools.github_description: CryoREAD de novo EM modeler.

tools.name: DeepFold-EM
tools.usage: Refining protein coordinates in low-resolution Cryo-EM maps combining deep networks with physics refinement.
tools.strengths: Stabilizes secondary structure conformations, fits large assemblies accurately.
tools.weaknesses: High GPU compute required for iterative conformation searches.
tools.github_description: DeepFold-EM structures.

tools.name: CryoFold
tools.usage: Fitting AlphaFold predictions to dynamic Cryo-EM map densities using differentiable simulation blocks.
tools.strengths: Captures alternative active/inactive states directly from raw density profiles.
tools.weaknesses: Inference time is high, requires careful input constraint setups.
tools.github_description: CryoFold density-fitter.

tools.name: AF2-EM
tools.usage: Rapid validation and scoring of AF2 structures against experimental Cryo-EM density maps.
tools.strengths: Identifies poorly folded domains or incorrect multimeric interfaces in predictions.
tools.weaknesses: Does not perform structural updates; validation-only tool.
tools.github_description: AF2-EM density validator.

tools.name: ISOLDE
tools.usage: Interactive molecular dynamics flexible fitting of AF2 coordinates into crystallographic or Cryo-EM maps.
tools.strengths: Allows real-time manual editing of structures while physics fields prevent bond overlap.
tools.weaknesses: Requires visual monitoring; not designed for automated high-throughput execution.
tools.github_description: ISOLDE interactive fitting.

tools.name: Phaser-AlphaFold
tools.usage: Using AlphaFold models directly for molecular replacement calculations inside Phaser solver.
tools.strengths: Integrates error estimates (pLDDT) into the likelihood targets for search templates.
tools.weaknesses: Templates require truncation of low-confidence loops beforehand.
tools.github_description: Phaser molecular replacement.

tools.name: MrBump
tools.usage: Automated pipeline for molecular replacement using search models retrieved from AlphaFold DB.
tools.strengths: Saves hours of search preparation, pipelines multiple domains automatically.
tools.weaknesses: Dependent on the presence of related structural domains in databases.
tools.github_description: MrBump automated pipeline.

tools.name: AMPLE
tools.usage: Molecular replacement using clusters of truncated ab initio/AlphaFold structures.
tools.strengths: Solves structures with no known template structure homologs.
tools.weaknesses: Computationally intensive; requires trying many cluster variations.
tools.github_description: AMPLE molecular replacement.

tools.name: SIMBAD
tools.usage: Sequence-independent molecular replacement scanning crystallographic database models.
tools.strengths: Solves structures when the sequence annotation is incorrect or missing.
tools.weaknesses: Requires searching thousands of models, high computation cost.
tools.github_description: SIMBAD independent MR.

tools.name: Modelcraft
tools.usage: Automated protein model building combining network coordinates with density fitting protocols.
tools.strengths: End-to-end building, drastically limits manual adjustment requirements.
tools.weaknesses: Lacks precision on heavily disordered chain regions.
tools.github_description: Modelcraft automated building.

tools.name: AlphaFold DB
tools.usage: Accessing over 200 million 3D protein structure predictions freely available online.
tools.strengths: Massive repository covering human proteome and 48 key model organisms, easy programmatic access.
tools.weaknesses: Contains unrefined predictions, lacks small molecule ligands, nucleic acids, and cofactor annotations.
tools.github_description: Open source code for AlphaFold 2.

github_top_forks.name: bjornwallner/alphafoldv2.2.0
github_top_forks.description: Open source code for AlphaFold.

citing_papers.authors: Mihály Váradi, Stephen Anyango et al.

tools.name: AlphaFill
tools.usage: Enriching AlphaFold DB models by transplanting missing ligands, cofactors, and metal ions from homologous structures.
tools.strengths: Rebuilds biological context for apo-predictions, calculates local quality alignment score.
tools.weaknesses: Transplant-based modeling can introduce steric clashes or incorrect positions in dynamic pockets.
tools.github_description: Enriching AlphaFold DB models by transplanting missing ligands, cofactors, and metal ions.

citing_papers.title: AlphaFill: enriching AlphaFold models with ligands and cofactors

tools.name: αCharges
tools.usage: Calculation and visualization of partial atomic charges for AlphaFold DB models.
tools.strengths: Provides crucial electrostatic charge annotations for structural models, uses rapid empirical methods.
tools.weaknesses: Accuracy depends heavily on the quality of the underlying AlphaFold DB coordinate model.
tools.github_description: αCharges: partial atomic charge calculations on AlphaFold models.

citing_papers.title: αCharges: a database of partial atomic charges for AlphaFold Library models

tools.name: ESM Atlas
tools.usage: Database containing over 600 million predicted structures from metagenomic sequence samples.
tools.strengths: Massive structural expansion, covers dark matter of sequence databases.
tools.weaknesses: Predictions are single-chain only; lacks multimeric context.
tools.github_description: ESM Metagenomic Atlas.

tools.name: AlphaFill DB
tools.usage: Freely accessible database containing pre-computed ligand-enriched coordinates for millions of AF models.
tools.strengths: Instantly accessible, resolves binding site layouts for drug design.
tools.weaknesses: Limited to pocket annotations matching experimental structures.
tools.github_description: AlphaFill precomputed database.

tools.name: Foldcomp DB
tools.usage: Accessing compressed structural libraries of the complete AlphaFold Database to stream locally.

tools.name: Fpocket-AFDB

tools.github_description: Fpocket database annotations.

tools.name: PocketMiner
tools.usage: Rapid machine learning classifier to identify cryptic druggable pockets in AlphaFold models.
tools.strengths: Extremely fast, identifies pockets requiring small conformational shifts.
tools.weaknesses: Accuracy dependent on local structure quality scores (pLDDT).
tools.github_description: PocketMiner pocket prediction.

tools.name: PocketOmics
tools.usage: Mapping human genomic variants onto binding pockets of AlphaFold models to infer functional disruption.
tools.strengths: Connects missense variants directly with structural drug pockets.
tools.weaknesses: Reliant on accuracy of pocket borders.
tools.github_description: PocketOmics genomic map.

tools.name: ESMFold DB
tools.usage: Precomputed structures of standard protein databases generated via ESMFold.
tools.strengths: Fast retrieval of standard families.
tools.weaknesses: Lacks custom multimeric assemblies.

tools.name: ChimeraX-AlphaFold
tools.usage: Interactive interface inside UCSF ChimeraX to fetch, display, and color structures by pLDDT confidence scores.

tools.strengths: Provides fast screening feedback during design.

tools.name: LIVIA

social_posts.text: Alphafoldology is moving fast! Just reviewed RoseTTAFold-All-Atom. Predicting small molecules, RNA/DNA, and modified proteins all in one open framework. A massive leap for structural biology.
Need to search millions of AlphaFold structures? Foldseek does structural alignment in milliseconds. Essential tool for the downstream #alphafoldology research pipeline.
ColabFold has reached a huge milestone in accelerating structural biology. By pairing MMseqs2 fast search with AF2, we've enabled researchers worldwide to predict structures in minutes.
